calls.name: Tata Consultancy Services
calls.reasoning: TCS has fallen ~34% YTD and recently touched a 52-week low of ₹2,110, reflecting sector-wide pressure from aggressive AI model launches (Anthropic Claude) threatening traditional IT services revenue. Near-term positives exist — a multimillion-euro Canada Life AI deal, a new GVIC business unit, and an Anthropic partnership — but these have not reversed the structural valuation de-rating; the stock is barely above its 52-week low with no clear catalyst for a one-day outperformance over the index. Short-term direction is close to a coin flip, and no scored directional calls have been made yet, so confidence stays low.

calls.name: Jupiter Wagons
calls.reasoning: Q4 FY26 results were materially weak — net profit down 72% YoY and revenue down 25% YoY — creating near-term sentiment overhang; the stock appears to have slipped below your ₹276 level. The long-term story (₹4,675 cr order book, BESS diversification) remains intact but the poor quarterly print and absence of any scored directional track record here demands caution. Short-term direction is close to a coin flip and this note is information only, not advice.

calls.name: Aditya Infotech (CP PLUS)
calls.reasoning: Q4 FY26 results were strong — revenue up ~45.5% YoY and PAT up ~208% YoY — with management raising FY27 guidance to ₹6,000–6,500 Cr, and the stock has recently pulled back ~4.3% from your buy price. However, the stock is already up significantly post-IPO (Aug 2025) and a new JV (Corelink, cable manufacturing, June 10) adds execution risk; short-term direction after a post-results run is genuinely unpredictable, and no new near-term catalyst is evident today.

calls.name: Vedanta
calls.reasoning: The demerger of 4 entities (Aluminium, Power, Oil & Gas, Iron & Steel) has now completed and listed with mixed debut — Aluminium opened strongly but Oil & Gas hit a lower circuit, making net value impact for VEDL holders unclear day-to-day. Q4 FY26 profits surged ~92% YoY and a ₹7/share interim dividend (record date June 24) provides near-term income support, but parent Vedanta Resources' heavy debt load remains an overhang. Short-term direction is essentially a coin flip at this stage; no scored track record here warrants any confidence. This is information only, not financial advice.
keyNews: 4 demerged entities listed: Aluminium Metal strong (+ve), Oil & Gas hits 5% lower circuit (mixed debut)
Q4 FY26 net profit up ~92% YoY to ₹6,698 Cr
